early_childhood.photo_description: Child me looking at a cake
early_childhood.dialogue: Mama's homemade blueberry jam cake for every birthday of mine. I didn’t know the sweetness was a distraction.
Some things were easier to fix with a black crayon than a real conversation.
Hold on, buddy. We’ll just keep sewing the pieces back together like always.

early_childhood.photo_description: A card with one or two tear-drop stains
early_childhood.dialogue: I wrote it perfectly. I thought if the letters didn't shake, the house wouldn't either. How naïve.

teens.dialogue: The sound stayed in the walls long after the ceramic shards were quickly swept away.
If I wasn't the best to anyone, I was invisible. This was when I decided to be someone.
My first heartbreak. When I realised love was just a draft you could delete.
I wasn't really keeping anyone out. I was just making sure I had a place of my own to hide.

twenties.dialogue: The future could wait a little longer. I was too busy chasing the feeling of having one.
Art doesn't care about rent. Or so I told myself...
I was in the room… but I was already looking for the exit.
A beautiful view. I just didn't notice the grime on my own side of the glass.

thirties.dialogue: Money didn't make me better. It just made my excuses even more expensive.
We were side-by-side for a time… but we weren't holding on like I hoped.
I needed the world to see my gold, so they wouldn't look at the papers.
Even when I stayed, I kept the door unlocked. Just in case.

forty.dialogue: The math finally caught up to me. You can't borrow a life forever.
I should really do something about that. Or maybe it’s too late?
The world was screaming at me. I’ve only forgotten how to answer.
I used to be so afraid of the dark. Now, I’m just too tired to fix it.

end_game.ending_dialogue: I spent forty years learning how to leave. I’ve mastered the art of the exit, the scribbled-out name and the half-packed bag. But looking at all these pieces... for the first time, I think I’d rather see what happens if I stay. Even if it's broken. Even if the stitching is clumsy.
